intro: Pronephrium cuspidatum, also known as the cusp-fronded shield fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and pain, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidant, and anti-bacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pronephrium cuspidatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In herbal medicine, Pronephrium cuspidatum is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections.
